steps.title: Identify the needed evidence
steps.content: The claim is that Newland "visualizes the details of a future with May." The best quotation must show Newland mentally imagining what will happen later with May, not simply noticing the opera setting or observing May in the present.

steps.title: Eliminate present details
steps.content: Choice A describes Newland seeing May touch flowers, so it is present-moment observation. Choice B describes Newland turning from the stage and scanning the opposite side of the house, which is also present action. Choice C describes the old Academy of Music and the fashionable audience's attitude toward it, not Newland's future with May.

steps.title: Choose the future image
steps.content: Choice D says Newland's imagination leaps ahead of an engagement ring, a betrothal kiss, and a wedding march, then pictures May at his side. Those details directly show him visualizing a future with May. The answer is D.
